title: pyrus bucharica var. typica
intro: Pyrus bucharica var. typica, also known as the Bactrian pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Bactrian pear is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Pyrus bucharica var. typica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
